title: Insufficient Praise

jokes.text: I don't have polio, asshole. - Every kid with those kind of gloves had polio.
jokes.setup: Larry explains his childhood baseball failures and Leon suggests it's because of polio
jokes.punchline: Larry denies having polio, Leon insists all kids with those gloves had polio
jokes.explanation: Absurd leap in logic delivered with complete confidence - peak Leon character comedy

jokes.text: Poo-poo-be-doop? - If I lived in the fuckin' '40s... I'd know I'm tappin' that ass.
jokes.setup: Discussion about 1940s expressions and what women used to say
jokes.punchline: Leon's crude interpretation of innocent vintage expressions
jokes.explanation: Classic Leon - taking wholesome nostalgia and making it sexual

jokes.text: Fuckin' in black and white.
jokes.setup: Leon reflecting on romantic life in the 1940s
jokes.punchline: Reduces the entire era to this visual/sexual concept
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and a uniquely Leon perspective - visual absurdity compressed into four words

jokes.text: That's like Mary Ann from fuckin' Gilligan's Island.
jokes.setup: Larry receives a sex doll and Leon examines it
jokes.punchline: Leon's specific pop culture reference for the doll's appearance
jokes.explanation: Good character beat - Leon's unexpected cultural knowledge applied to sex dolls

jokes.text: The new ones, they got one that's sensitive around the fuckin' neck. You could put hickeys on that motherfucker if you wanted to, man.
jokes.setup: Discussion of sex doll technology improvements
jokes.punchline: Leon's disturbingly specific knowledge of advanced features
jokes.explanation: Escalates the discomfort with Leon's casual expertise in this area

jokes.text: The pee cube. You pee into the cube, and there's a chute that goes down. Maybe we can get a... create a vacuum or something, sucking the urine down the chute.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining his revolutionary urinal design
jokes.punchline: The overly complicated 'pee cube' solution to splashing
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry over-engineering a simple problem with absurd technological solutions

jokes.text: There's no defecation in the building. - Could you do that? - I could put a sensor on there that detects a penis.
jokes.setup: Larry wants to prevent defecation in his coffee shop bathroom
jokes.punchline: Contractor matter-of-factly suggests a penis detection sensor
jokes.explanation: Escalates Larry's bathroom obsession to technological absurdity

jokes.text: Doot-doot-doot. 'Penis detected.' Woot. The door will open up, and then once the man is done, doot-doot-doot. Woot. Slide down.
jokes.setup: Explaining how the penis sensor would work
jokes.punchline: Sound effects and choreography for the detection system
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy through sound effects and the contractor's earnest technical explanation

jokes.text: You think you're up for this fight, General Lee? - Well, I do declare. As I recall, General Lee was a loser.
jokes.setup: Ted Danson calls Larry 'General Lee' as a Civil War taunt
jokes.punchline: Larry's deadpan historical correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry response - deflating Ted's dramatic moment with factual pedantry

jokes.text: Oh, hey, Clive. This is, uh, Larry David, a good friend of Jeff. Uh, I saw the show last night. I really enjoyed it. Uh, good job.
jokes.setup: Jeff forces Larry to leave praise voicemail for Clive's performance
jokes.punchline: Larry's completely flat, unenthusiastic 'praise'
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's central conflict - Larry's inability to give proper praise

jokes.text: Jesus fucking Christ, you're amazing! - You're gonna clean my makeup brushes. - Oh, my God. You're a fucking saint!
jokes.setup: Matilda does basic housework tasks
jokes.punchline: Susie's wildly over-the-top gratitude with profanity
jokes.explanation: Classic Susie character beat - excessive emotional response to mundane events

jokes.text: But keep Leon away from her, please. - That I can't guarantee.
jokes.setup: Susie warns Larry to keep Leon away from the new housekeeper
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate admission that he has no control over Leon
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff about Larry's living situation and Leon's unpredictability

jokes.text: You think I'm gonna fuck that doll? - Yeah, yeah. Have you ever played around with 'em? - No! - You cuddle it, whatever your journey is.
jokes.setup: Freddy defends sending Larry a sex doll
jokes.punchline: Freddy's casual 'whatever your journey is' philosophy
jokes.explanation: Freddy's new-age acceptance of Larry's potential doll usage

jokes.text: Maybe you should try necrophilia. - If I want to fuck a dead woman, I'll fuck the doll. Okay? And that way, I don't have to fuck a dead woman.
jokes.setup: Larry suggests necrophilia since Freddy is so open-minded
jokes.punchline: Freddy's twisted logic about dolls being a safe substitute for necrophilia
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that somehow makes twisted sense within Freddy's logic

jokes.text: All the free porn on the Internet. Why stick with magazines? It's on the Internet? Yeah. Do you have a computer? My kids bought me a computer, but I don't use it.
jokes.setup: Larry questions why Moke buys porn magazines
jokes.punchline: Moke's complete ignorance of internet porn despite owning a computer
jokes.explanation: Standard generational gap humor - older person oblivious to obvious technological convenience.

jokes.text: All the free porn on the Internet. Why stick with magazines? - It's on the Internet? - Yeah. Do you have a computer? - Yeah. My kids bought me a computer, but I don't use it.
jokes.setup: Larry questions why Moke buys porn magazines
jokes.punchline: Moke's complete ignorance of internet porn despite having a computer
jokes.explanation: Standard generational gap humor - sets up Moke's internet addiction storyline

jokes.text: He thinks people are aware that he dresses in black. He thinks he's like Johnny Cash.
jokes.setup: Larry defending his choice to always wear black
jokes.punchline: Friend's cutting observation about Larry's pretensions
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation that deflates Larry's attempt at mysterious cool with specific cultural comparison.

jokes.text: You're outvoted. - Yeah, I, I, I know, but she's cold, and it's worse to be cold, than hot. - No, it's actually worse to be hot than cold.
jokes.setup: Restaurant argument over turning on heat lamp
jokes.punchline: Philosophical debate over whether cold or hot is worse
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry getting into absurd debates over minor social situations

jokes.text: We're not snowmen. We need some heat.
jokes.setup: Argument about whether it's worse to be hot or cold
jokes.punchline: Larry's indignant declaration about not being snowmen
jokes.explanation: Larry's ridiculous comparison shows his tendency to escalate minor disputes with absurd analogies.

jokes.text: Here. Have her wear that. - Oh, that's funny, asshole.
jokes.setup: Larry asks for heat, patron suggests Carol wear his jacket
jokes.punchline: Carol's sarcastic response to the obvious non-solution
jokes.explanation: Simple comeback line - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I saw the lights of the train, and I... I heard the... the honk of the train, and my mom, she... she jumped onto the tracks, just really quickly, just to grab the stole, because... it meant so much to her.
jokes.setup: Carol tells elaborate story about her mother and a similar stole
jokes.punchline: The tragic subway death story that manipulates Larry into giving up the stole
jokes.explanation: Masterful manipulation scene that works on multiple levels when the truth is revealed

jokes.text: She's a professional crier. - What? A what? - She's a professional crier. She's hired to cry at weddings, and funerals, or sometimes TV shows.
jokes.setup: Larry asks what Carol does for work
jokes.punchline: The revelation that professional crying is an actual job
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for the episode's central scam - professional crier is genuinely surprising

jokes.text: She wanted Chinese, I wanted Italian. - So she wept openly and... - Oh. Yeah, and you went down... And you had Chinese food.
jokes.setup: Larry investigates Carol's crying patterns
jokes.punchline: Revealing that Carol uses crying to win restaurant arguments
jokes.explanation: Great detective work by Larry uncovering the pattern of manipulation

jokes.text: She fucking conned me out of that mink stole. This is incredible! - I don't have to buy your defense on this. - You saw it! You witnessed it. She's a con crier.
jokes.setup: Larry realizes he's been scammed
jokes.punchline: Larry's term 'con crier' and Jeff's refusal to believe it
jokes.explanation: 'Con crier' is a perfect portmanteau that captures the scam perfectly

jokes.text: She fucking conned me out of that mink stole. This is incredible! She's a con crier.
jokes.setup: Larry realizes Carol manipulated him with fake tears
jokes.punchline: His coining of the term 'con crier'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that perfectly captures the concept. 'Con crier' is instantly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: They're like, uh, big teddy bears. Yeah. - You collect big plastic dolls? - Yes, yes. I do. They, um... They normally come with clothes.
jokes.setup: Matilda discovers the sex doll
jokes.punchline: Larry's desperate attempt to explain it as a collectible
jokes.explanation: Standard Larry panic lie that makes everything worse

jokes.text: Like the American Girl! Right, right. Yeah, they have a nurse's outfit, they have a flight attendant, a cheerleader. They even have, um, a, a maid!
jokes.setup: Larry tries to maintain his doll collecting lie
jokes.punchline: His increasingly specific and inappropriate costume descriptions
jokes.explanation: Larry digging himself deeper with increasingly obvious sexual costume options

jokes.text: Her mouth. She's like 'F' this, 'F' that. She's so vulgar. Like, F-bomb for everything.
jokes.setup: Matilda explains why she's quitting Susie
jokes.punchline: The ultra-polite way she describes Susie's profanity
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Matilda's propriety vs Susie's vulgarity

jokes.text: Oh, Sherry? You gave it a name? - Yeah, I mean, I got her out of the box. Give her a little air and shit. - You didn't try and get a little friendly with her, did you?
jokes.setup: Larry discovers Leon named the sex doll
jokes.punchline: Larry's growing suspicion about Leon's relationship with 'Sherry'
jokes.explanation: Sets up the Leon-doll relationship that pays off later

jokes.text: I wouldn't put it past you, let's just say that. - I had a lady give me a wedgie with my own goddamn johnson.
jokes.setup: Larry and Leon argue about Leon's character
jokes.punchline: Leon's completely inappropriate sexual boast as a defense
jokes.explanation: Peak Leon absurdity - turning an accusation into an opportunity to brag

jokes.text: Be cool, Sherry.
jokes.setup: Larry demands Leon get rid of the sex doll
jokes.punchline: Leon's tender goodbye to the doll
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leon moment - treating an inanimate object with genuine affection

jokes.text: You breached. You made a solemn promise, a vow, and you fucking breached, asshole.
jokes.setup: Susie discovers Larry stole her housekeeper
jokes.punchline: Treating housekeeper sharing like a legal contract violation
jokes.explanation: Classic Susie escalation - making domestic arrangements into dramatic betrayal

jokes.text: I can put my hands in boiling, scalding water, and I don't feel anything. I don't need gloves! - Let me see those hands. - You've never washed a dish in your life.
jokes.setup: Larry claims to be a great dishwasher with special heat resistance
jokes.punchline: Jeff's immediate debunking of Larry's ridiculous claim
jokes.explanation: Larry's delusional self-image meets reality - classic character comedy

jokes.text: Clive seems to think it was insufficient praise. - What? Insufficient praise? - Yeah. You weren't enthusiastic.
jokes.setup: Jeff explains why Clive was offended by the voicemail
jokes.punchline: The absurd concept of 'insufficient praise' as a formal complaint
jokes.explanation: Perfect episode title moment - captures the social pressure to perform enthusiasm

jokes.text: Hi. This is Larry David. I just want to say I really enjoyed the show. Good job.' - Yeah. Like I said, I'm not, I'm not good with... If it was in person, I would have been much more effusive.
jokes.setup: Clive repeats Larry's flat voicemail back to him
jokes.punchline: Larry's weak excuse about being better in person
jokes.explanation: Hearing the voicemail again emphasizes how terrible Larry's praise was

jokes.text: A house divided against itself cannot stand.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to prove he can give proper praise
jokes.punchline: His Lincoln impression as an example of enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Larry's random historical impression as proof of his enthusiastic capabilities is awkwardly off-topic.

jokes.text: Mmm! Mmm! Mmm! Mmm-mmm! Oh, my God. Oh. This is unbelievable! This Metzler's? Best, the best. You see that? That is genuine praise.
jokes.setup: Larry tastes a sandwich while Clive watches
jokes.punchline: Larry's over-the-top enthusiasm for food vs his flat praise for Clive's acting
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic juxtaposition - Larry can praise sandwiches but not human achievement

jokes.text: That's for sandwiches, a well-struck iron, a good golf shot.
jokes.setup: Clive noting Larry can show genuine praise
jokes.punchline: Larry's specific categories for appropriate enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic - enthusiasm reserved for mundane pleasures, not human artistic achievement.

jokes.text: One wonders why somebody would do it. - Is he for real? - No, I'm just saying. It seems... a little crazy.
jokes.setup: Larry questions why someone would do a one-man show
jokes.punchline: Larry's complete inability to stop making it worse
jokes.explanation: Larry's complete social obliviousness reaching peak cringe

jokes.text: He's fucking a doll. - What? - I delivered a blow-up fuck doll to his house. How could you possibly know that? - The package said, 'Do not puncture. Discreet delivery.' Mailmen know.
jokes.setup: Mailman tells Ted about Larry's sex doll delivery
jokes.punchline: Professional insight into what 'discreet delivery' packages actually contain
jokes.explanation: 'Mailmen know' is a great insight into service worker awareness

jokes.text: Your ex-husband, he's fucking... a sex doll, just so you know. He's basically sticking his penis in a balloon.
jokes.setup: Cheryl visits with information about Larry
jokes.punchline: Her blunt, clinical description of sex doll usage
jokes.explanation: Cheryl's matter-of-fact delivery makes the crude description funnier

jokes.text: Did I give that stole to... real Carol? Or did I give it to, you know, the professional crier?
jokes.setup: Larry confronts Carol about being a professional crier
jokes.punchline: His philosophical distinction between 'real Carol' and 'professional crier'
jokes.explanation: Smart way to frame the authenticity question at the heart of the scam

jokes.text: Who knows what else you've cried your way into. The couch, the coffee table, the painting. Maybe the house.
jokes.setup: Larry realizing Carol might have conned her way into everything
jokes.punchline: His paranoid inventory of her possessions ending with 'maybe the house'
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Larry's paranoid mind reconstructing Carol's entire life as one big con.

jokes.text: You created a real problem with Uncle Moke with the Internet porn. - It's all day. It's a problem. It's all he does. And he is paying. He's doing the chat rooms now.
jokes.setup: Larry's helpful internet porn tip backfired
jokes.punchline: Moke's complete addiction to online porn and chat rooms
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing unintended consequences of Larry's advice

jokes.text: Any motherfucker that come up to that shit, with a little-ass penis, of course it works for them. But you got a big-ass johnson, you gotta stand a little further back, see, so your johnson don't overshoot and hit the back of that motherfucker.
jokes.setup: Leon tests Larry's urinal prototype
jokes.punchline: Leon's technical critique based on penis size considerations
jokes.explanation: Leon bringing his unique perspective to Larry's bathroom engineering

jokes.text: Hire her for opening night. Let her sit in the audience, she'll sob away. Once she starts sobbing, everyone else will start sobbing. Sobs are contagious.
jokes.setup: Jeff needs to help Clive's confidence for opening night
jokes.punchline: Larry's scheme to hire the professional crier to manipulate the audience
jokes.explanation: Brilliant solution that weaponizes the professional crier concept

jokes.text: Ah. There. She's gonna start. - Look out. It's really catching on now.
jokes.setup: Carol begins crying at Clive's performance
jokes.punchline: Larry and Jeff watching their manipulation work in real-time
jokes.explanation: Satisfying payoff to the scheme with Larry and Jeff as puppet masters

jokes.text: The more I watched you, the more I'm convinced that what you did at the table at lunch that day was total bullshit. Your mother didn't fall into the subway tracks. That's a cock-and-bull story if I ever heard one.
jokes.setup: Larry confronts Carol after watching her professional crying
jokes.punchline: Larry's complete conviction that the subway story was fake
jokes.explanation: Good detective moment where Larry uses evidence to confront the con

jokes.text: Crying? - No, that's crying. That's not crying. - No, she's not! - I'm crying! I'm crying, Larry! - I paid her to cry like this!
jokes.setup: Police intervene in Larry and Carol's stole fight
jokes.punchline: Confusion over whether Carol is genuinely crying or professionally crying
jokes.explanation: Great confusion over the nature of Carol's tears in the moment

jokes.text: You said you were going to deflate it. - You know what? I didn't get around to it. I'll... Slipped my mind. - You're fuckin' her, aren't you? - I'm not gonna sit here and let you do this shit to me right now.
jokes.setup: Larry discovers Leon still hasn't gotten rid of Sherry
jokes.punchline: Leon's defensive reaction when directly accused
jokes.explanation: Leon's indignant reaction confirms Larry's suspicion

jokes.text: Be good, Sherry.
jokes.setup: Larry about to deflate the sex doll Leon has grown attached to
jokes.punchline: Larry adopting Leon's affectionate farewell to the doll
jokes.explanation: Larry's unexpected moment of sentiment toward the doll shows even he was affected by Leon's attachment.

jokes.text: Asco! - No. No. Basta! - Eres un pervertido. - No, no, no! I quit! You're worse than Miss Susie!
jokes.setup: Matilda walks in on Larry deflating the sex doll
jokes.punchline: Matilda's horror and declaration that Larry is worse than Susie
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback - Larry being worse than the notoriously vulgar Susie

jokes.text: Clive Owen's portrayal of Thor Heyerdahl is as nuanced, varied, and moving as the ocean he traversed.
jokes.setup: Reading the positive review from opening night
jokes.punchline: The flowery praise Larry could never give, achieved through manipulation
jokes.explanation: Ironic that artificial manipulation achieved the genuine praise Larry couldn't give

jokes.text: When you were crying in the play, was that, was that a job or was that real? - No. No, it was real. I mean, it was 10% more, because that's what I'm paid, but I would have cried...
jokes.setup: Clive discovers Carol is a professional crier
jokes.punchline: Carol's admission that she added '10% more' crying because she was paid
jokes.explanation: Perfect detail about how professional performance enhances genuine emotion

jokes.text: It was 10% more, because that's what I'm paid, but I would have cried... Did somebody pay you? It was... Did Jeff pay you?
jokes.setup: Carol trying to explain her crying at the play was mostly real
jokes.punchline: Clive's mounting realization that their entire relationship was a business transaction
jokes.explanation: Devastating revelation that destroys Clive's romantic relationship through the discovery of professional manipulation.

jokes.text: People hate surprise parties. - No, no, no, no. - That's fun? That's fun? - Yes.
jokes.setup: Larry plans a surprise party for Jeff
jokes.punchline: Leon's incredulous reaction to the concept of surprise parties
jokes.explanation: End-of-episode setup for next storyline - Leon's practical wisdom vs Larry's social conventions

tmdb_overview: Marty Funkhauser's brother sends Larry an unusual gift; and an actor friend of the Greens is dissatisfied with Larry's praise of his performance.
